title: pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dysenterica
intro: Pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dysenterica, also known as common fleabane, is a plant species in the daisy family with small yellow flowers and a distinctive, pungent aroma. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dysenterica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
